title: COMS Program Review 2018 (Part 1)
text:
Program Review/CQUIP 2018 COMS
Program Review—Communication Studies (COMS)—2018-19
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
2017-18 was the year that the only full time faculty member in the COMS program was
on sabbatical leave. While there was a full time leave replacement, the number of
offered sections dropped significantly from previous years and only 15 sections were
offered for the entire year. This is down from the +/- 22 sections offered per year for
the past decade. Further affecting this dramatic decrease is the growing demand placed
on COMS to meet the General Education requirements as they enhanced through the
GenEd restructuring and the Interstate Passport.
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
For SLO assessment, this year (18-19) is deemed the “initialization year” in the SLO
manual, so faculty are supposed to work with the SLO committee to create program
maps, make any necessary changes to program-level outcomes, and develop their first
key assessment (to be implemented next year. Unfortunately, there is no currently-
functioning SLO committee.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
As noted above, COMS has significantly increased demand without the ability to meet
said demand. There exists a very-limited pool of part-time faculty with the necessary
education to teach in this field. Currently, we have one PT faculty member who is
willing to teach 2-3 sections per year, another who is willing to teach at maximum
capacity but is actively looking for a full time position elsewhere, and 2 new people who
have recently moved into the area. One is only willing to teach the Mass Media class
and one who has no experience teaching at the college level and needs to slowly ramp
up. Assuming that all these individuals remain in place we still do not have the capacity
to meet the General education requirements, much less support the vibrant major we
have developed. Research into student majors determined that COMS has become the
most popular, single-subject, academic major.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Submit a CQIP if you
are requesting a budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly
budget allocations).
COMS faculty pay for themselves—we have the students to fill the seats and we teach a
5-5 load, so we generate a substantial FTES, while our students also have time in their
schedules to accomplish 15 units per term. With increased GE demand, we need to
ensure students can meet their needs here at COS.

-- 1 of 4 --

Program Review/CQUIP 2018 COMS
While an additional full time faculty member is always an expense, the increased FTES
generated more than covers that expense. Additionally, if students leave campus to
complete their education, we lose even more FTES as well as harming our degree-
completion numbers.
Furthermore, the new funding formula incentivizes degree completion, especially AA-T
degree completion. COMS is a leader in that area as well as being a popular second
major, allowing COS to receive double the credit.

-- 2 of 4 --

title: COMS Program Review 2018 (Part 2)
text:
Program Review/CQUIP 2018 COMS
Operational Unit Program Review
Continuous Quality Improvement Proposal (CQIP)
Resource: New Faculty Position
Operational:
Board Policy? Yes  No Administrative Procedure?  Yes No
Fiscal:  One-time Funds  Ongoing Funding
Issue: Briefly describe the issue.
Mission critical need for one additional full-time COMS faculty member.
Vision/Mission/Institutional Master Plan Alignment: Address if this proposal is
aligned with the College’s vision, mission, and/or a strategic goal within the Institutional
Master Plan. Usually these proposals have quoted a portion of one or more of areas
being aligned with.
COS provides accessible, flexible, affordable, and innovative education leading to associate
degrees, certificates, college transfer, career and technical education….
COS provides:
• Rigorous and comprehensive transfer programs
• General education programs
• Workforce training and certification
Excellence – Our decisions and actions reflect our commitment to accountability, innovation,
and learning.
Goal #1: Promote and support educational goal completion for all students.
Goal #2: Sustain institutional health and vitality to meet the needs of the community.
Program Review Objective: Identify Program Review Objective/Effort if applicable.
Background: Describe the background on this issue – where we are at the present time
and how we got here.
The only way to stabilize both the COMS program and COSGE, is to hire a new, FT
Communication Studies Faculty Member. This will provide both stabilization while also
allowing for growth in COMS majors.
Background: Describe the background on this issue – where we are at the present time
and how we got here.
For the past decade, the COMS program has become increasingly vigorous as both a GE
program and a major. For 2014 through 2017, we averaged 21 sections per year. A deep
dive into the numbers shows that we averaged 11 sections a term, with most of those
classes enrolled to within 3 of maximum capacity. When only the sections taught by a
full time faculty member are included, 78% of the sections were near –to- above
capacity. In 2017, the COSGE pattern was amended to ensure that all graduates would
have successful completed a COMS course.

-- 3 of 4 --

title: COMS Program Review 2018 (Part 3)
text:
Program Review/CQUIP 2018 COMS
Rationale: Explain the reasoning behind the proposed improvements. Budgetary Impact:
Detail any funding impact to the proposed improvements; sometimes this is a reduction
in expenses, and sometimes there is increased spending needs to accomplish the
improvements (e.g., a capacity issue within a programmatic area). However, sometimes
the proposed improvement has no budgetary impact as it is a process or procedure
improvement that does not have a financial component to it. Please explain and justify
the extent of the impact(s) here.
Unlike other GenEd areas, there are no other courses that satisfy the general education
requirements and there are only a tiny number of individuals who are able to teach the
core courses.
Resource Allocation Adjustments (if any): Like with fiscal impacts, process and
procedure improvements can require changes in how resources on campus are allocated;
it could be streamlining people’s time, or tasks, or workflows, etc. Or the CQIP is
addressing capacity issues for a programmatic area; in these circumstances there is
likely both a budgetary impact AND resource allocation adjustment. These adjustments
are detailed here.
As this new hire will generate substantial FTES--more than enough to cover salary and
benefits, this hire will improve positive cash flow.
Recommendation: The recommendation is usually to implement the proposed
improvement, and this gives a chance to briefly summarize the issue and its positive
impact. In matters of budgetary increase requests this is a good place to reiterate and
document the justification.
A new, Full Time, COMS faculty member must be hired. Hiring an additional FT
Communication Studies Faculty member will mean that our students can complete their
GE in a timely fashion and we can continue to offer this most-popular major. If we fail
to take this action, a significant number of students will not be able to meet their
educational goals. The choice is clear: meet demand and grow FTES or motivate
students to get their needs met elsewhere.
Submitted By: _Jayne Turk__________________ Date: 12/17/2018_______
Action Taken: __________________________________________________
